authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Webber Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Webber Lake not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Webber Lake & Lacey Meadows — Truckee Donner Land Trust
sources.quotedText: Webber Lake Lacey Meadows are open to the public from dawn to dusk for day use. Camping, fires, and motorized vehicles are prohibited on the property except in the campground.

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R2 Sierra Webber Lake Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Sub-catchable Put and Grow June-September; R2 Sierra Webber Lake Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Super-catchable Put and Take June-September; R2 Sierra Webber Lake Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Fingerling Put and Grow June-September

sources.title: Lahontan Cutthroat Trout | California Department of Fish and Wildlife
sources.quotedText: In California, the historic range of Lahontan cutthroat trout includes Lake Tahoe and the Carson, Truckee, and Walker river basins.

sources.title: Everything You Need To Know About Webber Lake — Tahoe Quarterly
sources.quotedText: Its waters still teem with trophy trout—now supplemented by recently planted Lahontan cutthroats.

parameters.note: Webber Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Webber Lake / Lacey Meadows is Truckee Donner Land Trust property open to the public only from dawn to dusk for day use; camping, fires, and motorized vehicles are prohibited outside the designated campground.

regulations.citation: TDLT Webber Lake & Lacey Meadows — Rules, Regulations, Good to Know
regulations.humanSummary: Day-use access only, dawn to dusk; no camping/fires/motorized vehicles outside the campground
regulations.verbatimText: Webber Lake Lacey Meadows are open to the public from dawn to dusk for day use. Camping, fires, and motorized vehicles are prohibited on the property except in the campground.

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Webber Lake (Region 2, Sierra Co.) for sub-catchable-size Lahontan Cutthroat Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of June-September. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Webber Lake (Region 2, Sierra Co.) for super-catchable-size Lahontan Cutthroat Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of June-September. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Webber Lake (Region 2, Sierra Co.) for fingerling-size Lahontan Cutthroat Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of June-September. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
